sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Stanley Hills LLC sells VisionWave Holdings (VWAV) stock for $8.2m
feed.summary: Stanley Hills LLC, a significant owner of VisionWave Holdings (NASDAQ:VWAV), recently sold over 600,000 shares for approximately $8.2 million, decreasing its stake. Despite a strong six-month price return, VisionWave faces financial challenges, including a negative P/E ratio and low current ratio, alongside high price volatility. The company, however, is expanding its technological and commercial presence with new intellectual property, strategic partnerships, and defense technology initiatives.

feed.title: ASP Isotopes Makes Strategic Equity Investment in Opeongo
feed.summary: ASP Isotopes Inc. (ASPI) has made a strategic equity investment in biotechnology company Opeongo, Inc., purchasing over 4.3 million Series Seed-1 preferred shares on January 26, 2026. This investment provides ASP Isotopes with a significant stake in Opeongo, which develops therapeutics for fibrosis, inflammation, and cancer, and secures key investor rights including governance, veto, and a right of first offer on medical isotope supply for Opeongo's future drug programs. Despite rapid revenue growth, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates ASPI as Neutral due to weak financial fundamentals like net losses and high leverage.

feed.title: Big Beat, Bigger Plans: AEP Stock Powers Up on Data Center Boom
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) saw its shares rise over 2% after reporting a Q4 earnings beat and issuing a strong outlook for 2026-2028, driven by the increasing demand from data centers. The company plans significant capital investments, with a focus on grid modernization and renewables, to support this growth and achieve its long-term earnings per share growth target. Analysts have responded positively, reiterating bullish ratings due to AEP's strategic position in the energy transition.

feed.title: Cisco Climbs Toward Highs While IREN Faces 10% Reality Check
feed.summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO) is steadily approaching new highs, driven by institutional demand for AI infrastructure, while IREN Limited (IREN) is experiencing a sharp pullback. Cisco benefits from its role as a stable "picks-and-shovels" provider for enterprise AI, focusing on secure networking and long-term partnerships. In contrast, IREN's rapid rally fueled by an AI pivot and Microsoft deal is facing scrutiny due to execution risks, valuation concerns, and the capital-intensive nature of AI infrastructure.

feed.title: Las Vegas tourism leaders urge patience as headwinds linger
feed.summary: Las Vegas tourism leaders acknowledge a dip in visitation and express concern over ongoing economic headwinds, including reduced international travel and consumer confidence. Despite the challenges, executives from Boyd Gaming and MGM Resorts International remain optimistic about a rebound, emphasizing the importance of consumer experience and infrastructure improvements. The article notes that while 2023 and 2024 were record years, 2025 saw reduced traffic and flatter gaming revenue, partially attributed to factors like the trade war and high prices.

feed.title: Down 20% From All-Time Highs, Can Apollo Stock Recover In 2026?
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management (APO) reported strong quarterly results with record combined fee and spread-related earnings, increasing adjusted net income to $1.4 billion. The company is experiencing significant growth in asset management and retirement services, driven by robust origination and expansion into new distribution channels. A valuation model projects APO stock could reach $181 by December 2027, implying a 35% total return from its current price of $134.
